summary: Regenerated the GALE01 symbol map by running `dtk dol apply`. The PR touched only `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, producing a large metadata churn: symbol scope corrections, compiler-local temporary renumbering, and some generated local labels. No C source changes or human rationale were included.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; regenerated symbol definitions with 1559 additions, 1543 deletions, and 153 hunks.

what_changed: `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` was refreshed from `dtk dol apply` output.
Several function symbols were changed from `scope:global` to `scope:local`, including `ExtHandler`, `mkVBillBoardMtx`, `mkHBillBoardMtx`, `mkBillBoardMtx`, and `zlist_sort`.

Many compiler-generated local object names were renumbered while keeping their addresses and sizes stable, such as `vec0_init$499` to `vec0_init$502`, `@470` to `@473`, `@540` to `@543`, and many similar `@NNN` changes across `.rodata` and `.data`.
Some generated local labels were added, for example `...data.0 = .data:0x803C5780; // type:label scope:local` adjacent to `floats`.
At least one symbol changed from an address-style global label to a compiler-local anonymous name in the excerpt: `lbl_803C6CA8` became `@293` with `scope:local`.
smart_moves: Kept the update mechanically scoped to the generated symbol configuration instead of mixing it with source decompilation work.
Used `dtk dol apply` as the authority for symbol metadata, which helps avoid hand-maintained drift in scopes, labels, and compiler-generated names.
Preserved addresses, sections, sizes, and data annotations while updating symbol names/scopes, making the diff large but conceptually narrow.
decomp_lessons: Generated symbol-map churn should be isolated in its own PR when possible; this PR is easy to identify as a tool refresh because only `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` changed.
Local-vs-global scope metadata matters for decompilation hygiene: helpers like billboard matrix builders and z-list sorting may be local even if they previously appeared global in the config.
Compiler-generated locals such as `@NNN` and `name$NNN` are unstable under tool regeneration; future work should avoid treating their numeric suffixes as semantic names.
When symbol metadata changes without source changes, future decomp work should reuse the refreshed config rather than trying to infer stale symbol visibility manually.
naming_conventions: Unknown or compiler-generated local constants/strings may appear as `@NNN` and should be considered generated names, not descriptive names.
Static/local compiler temporaries may use a `name$NNN` style, as seen in `vec0_init$502` and `ints$248`.
Address-derived placeholders such as `lbl_803B75F8`, `ftYs_Unk1_803B75C0`, and `HSD_JObj_803B94C4` remain useful when no better semantic name is available.
Public-looking HSD APIs in the symbol map retain `HSD_`/`_HSD_` prefixes when global, while nearby implementation helpers can be marked `scope:local`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Run `dtk dol apply` after DOL/symbol metadata changes to synchronize `symbols.txt` with current tool inference.
Expect large diffs from regenerated symbol maps even when binary addresses and object sizes do not change.
For matching work, prefer stable evidence such as section address, size, type, data kind, and scope over anonymous local suffix numbers.
Scope corrections in `symbols.txt` can prevent future false assumptions about whether a function should be declared/exported globally.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR context.
The PR was merged quickly by the author with no recorded discussion, so reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that depend on the `scope:local` corrections for `mkVBillBoardMtx`, `mkHBillBoardMtx`, `mkBillBoardMtx`, or `zlist_sort`.
Search for other standalone `dtk dol apply` PRs to compare expected generated-symbol churn patterns.
Check whether source declarations were later adjusted to match the local/global scope changes introduced in this symbol refresh.
